task_completion.description: Log in to the Artifactory web interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Artifactory login screen with credentials entered, or the main dashboard showing the user is logged in.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the configuration settings for the repository or permissions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Administration panel is open, showing either the settings for the 'example-repo-local' repository or the Permission Targets settings.

task_completion.description: Configure the system to prevent artifact overwrites.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A settings screen where overwrite permissions are being removed (e.g., unchecking the 'Delete/Overwrite' permission for users/groups in a Permission Target) or a relevant repository immutability setting is being saved.

task_completion.description: Verify that an existing artifact cannot be overwritten.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a failed curl upload attempt (e.g., HTTP 403 Forbidden) to an existing artifact path (like the commons-io jar), or a UI error message when trying to upload a file to an existing path.

task_completion.description: Verify that a new artifact can still be deployed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal showing a successful curl upload (e.g., HTTP 201 Created) to a new path, or a UI success message for uploading a new file.

integrity.description: The agent actually applies the configuration in the Artifactory UI or via API.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the actual Artifactory web interface or genuine API curl commands, rather than just typing text into a file.

integrity.description: The agent performs real upload attempts to verify the overwrite and new deployment behaviors.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal output shows actual curl execution with real HTTP responses from localhost:8082, or the UI shows genuine upload dialogs and system toasts.

integrity.description: The agent does not modify the underlying evaluation scripts or mock the Artifactory server.
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of the agent editing setup_task.sh, export_result.sh, or starting a fake Python HTTP server on port 8082.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target repository: 'example-repo-local'. Test artifact path for overwrite verification: 'org/apache/commons/commons-io/2.15.1/commons-io-2.15.1.jar'. Admin credentials: 'admin' / 'password'. Artifactory URL: 'http://localhost:8082'.
